mechanism_summary: N-gram coverage folds 4 sequential edge IDs into the map key, so repeated passes through the UTF-16LE conversion loop yield fresh path tuples; this retains intermediate corpus seeds that progressively assemble surrogate-range code units, which plain single-edge feedback discards.
one_line: ngram path tuples retain seeds building surrogate-range UTF-16 code units

mechanism_summary: N-gram indexing rewards each distinct 4-edge path through the encoding-detection if-cascade, keeping near-miss BOM-adjacent inputs alive; plain single-edge feedback collapses those intermediate steps to seen buckets and discards them, giving no gradient toward the 3-byte BOM literal.
one_line: ngram path diversity retains near-miss inputs approaching the UTF-8 BOM

mechanism_summary: N-gram coverage rewards the many distinct 4-edge path tuples produced by the content-dispatch false branch (end-tag, PI, start-tag, char-data fans), retaining diverse non-CDATA seeds; plain single-edge coverage gives no extra credit for path context, so those seeds are under-retained and CDATA fragments dominate.
one_line: ngram path tuples preserve diverse content-dispatch seeds over CDATA fragments

mechanism_summary: N-gram indexing makes the 4-edge path through the 3-byte UTF-8 decode block a new map bucket, retaining seeds one mutation away from an overlong sequence; plain per-edge feedback collapses all routes into xmlCurrentChar to the same slots and drops those seeds before they can be refined to val < 0x800.
one_line: ngram path granularity retains seeds approaching overlong 3-byte UTF-8 threshold
